text: so we did a diagnostic thoracoscopy on the left side first the left side lung was deflated with a double lumen et tube in c2 and once the thoracoscopy was done then we place the two other working port so that the adequate triangulation is achieved for the dissection of the adenoma in the anterior medial sternum so this is the location of the ectopic parathyroid which we confirm with the ct imaging
Now the other trocar were placed directly under vision and then we started dissecting the mediastinal pleura with the help of the harmonic scalpel.
One has to be very careful here so that no bleeding should be done which can jeopardize the surgery and the surrounding fat capsule of the adenoma should be removed in total without breaching it.
So we continue the dissection and then clip the vessels wherever it is necessary.
I'll just skip this dissection part.
So harmonic skeletal helps a lot and complete fat that is covering over the adenoma should be removed.
We should not breach the adenoma and it is mobilized all around from the mediastinum.
and then the superior side the vessels are clipped and divided and gland is completely freed from its attachment posteriorly also.
So once it is completely freed, we place a plastic bag inside the thorax and remove the complete adenoma.
And we make sure that proper hemostasis has been achieved.
There is no bleeding or oozing.
And then we place a chest tube under vision and place it superiorly.
